panels.description:
Shows the real-time and historical power state of each disk.
Standby = 0, Active/Idle = 1, Unknown = -1, Error = -2.
Useful for verifying spin-down behavior, monitoring disk usage patterns, and optimizing power consumption in NAS systems.
panels.title: Disk Power State (Standby vs Active)

panels.description:
Real-time CPU usage of each running Docker container across all monitored nodes: `media_vm`, `infra_vm`, and the Proxmox host.

This helps identify CPU-intensive containers (e.g. ffmpeg or qbittorrent) and track resource consumption trends across the system.

CPU usage is measured as a percentage of a single core (100% = 1 core fully used).

The container names are derived from Docker Compose service labels (e.g. qbittorrent, grafana) and grouped by host.
panels.title: Container CPU Usage (% per container across all nodes)

panels.description: Shows the amount of physical RAM currently in use on the Proxmox host, calculated as total memory minus available memory.
panels.title: RAM Usage

panels.description:
The percentage of CPU capacity used by each virtual machine (QEMU) and container (LXC) on the Proxmox node. 

It uses the `pve_cpu_usage_ratio` metric from the prometheus-pve-exporter, joined with guest metadata to label each line by guest name. 

CPU usage is shown relative to each guest’s allocated CPU quota. 

This helps identify resource-intensive guests and spot spikes or background activity across the virtualized environment.
panels.title: CPU Usage per VM/LXC

panels.description: Component temperatures collected via IPMI and smartctl, including CPU, memory, HDDs and chipset elements.
panels.title: Temperatures

panels.description:
Displays total CPU usage for the Proxmox node, calculated as 100% minus average idle time across all CPU cores.
Data is scraped via node_exporter and shown as a 1-minute rate.
panels.title: CPU Usage (%)

panels.description: Displays real-time RPM values for critical system fans reported via IPMI, including the CPU fan (CPU0_FAN) and system fans (SYS_FAN1, SYS_FAN3). This chart helps monitor cooling performance and detect potential fan failures or irregularities. Values are collected using ipmi_exporter at 15-second intervals.
panels.title: Fan Speeds

panels.description:
Memory usage per guest in percent. It calculates the ratio of pve_memory_usage_bytes to pve_memory_size_bytes. 

Identify memory-heavy workloads and monitor for over- or under-provisioned guests.
panels.title: RAM Usage per VM/LXC

targets.expr: sum by (id) (pve_up{instance="proxmox"} and on (id, instance) pve_guest_info)
sum by (id) (pve_cpu_usage_limit{instance="proxmox"} and on (id, instance) pve_guest_info)
sum by (id) (pve_memory_size_bytes{instance="proxmox"} and on (id, instance) pve_guest_info)
sum by (id) (pve_memory_usage_bytes{instance="proxmox",template!="1"} / pve_memory_size_bytes and on (id, instance) pve_guest_info and on (id) pve_up==1)
sum by (id) (pve_disk_size_bytes{instance="proxmox"} and on (id, instance) pve_guest_info)
sum by (id) (pve_disk_usage_bytes{instance="proxmox"} / pve_disk_size_bytes and on (id, instance) pve_guest_info{type="lxc"})
(rate(pve_network_receive_bytes{instance="proxmox"}[5m]) * on(id, instance) group_left(name, type) pve_guest_info) and on(id, instance) pve_up == 1
(rate(pve_network_transmit_bytes{instance="proxmox"}[5m])* on(id, instance) group_left(name, type) pve_guest_info) and on(id, instance) pve_up == 1
